spec_match.rationale: The demo now targets CSS url() referrer-policy() parsing and applies the selected policy to a background-image declaration.

interactive.rationale: Users select a policy, apply it to a CSS background request, inspect the generated declaration, and can compare the same endpoint with a JSON mirror.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page delivers the parent feature promise by showing per-resource referrer policy choices and their same-origin, cross-origin, and downgrade effects.

edge_cases.rationale: The policy table and live output cover no-referrer, same-origin, origin-only, strict-origin-when-cross-origin, unsafe-url, and downgrade behavior.

references.rationale: Provides direct reference links to the ChromeStatus entry, CSS Values 5 request URL modifiers section, and MDN Referrer-Policy documentation.

design_system.rationale: Uses shared CSS variables, includes accessible foreground colors for local button overrides, and replaces the generic copied probe with feature-specific checks.

summary: The request lab now centers on a CSS background-image declaration carrying referrer-policy(), with feature-specific CSS.supports probes and explicit fallback text for browsers without Chrome 150 support. Because the existing scoped echo route returns JSON rather than SVG, the page clearly separates the real CSS request declaration from the fetch-based JSON mirror used to display server payload shape.
